name: harmonic-weaver-stage
description: Headless Harmonic Weaver stage plane consumed by every UI and control client.
authority: This manifest is authoritative for WebSocket message names and payload shapes. Source Frame v1 and Instrument Control v1 remain authoritative on the OSC edges.

transport.encoding: UTF-8 JSON; one complete message object per text frame
transport.remote_access: Deployment concern; use authentication and TLS at a trusted reverse proxy.
transport.slow_client_rule: Each client has a bounded outbound queue. Disconnect it on overflow; never block the routing hot path.

hashes.algorithm: SHA-256 truncated to 128 bits, lowercase hex (32 characters)
hashes.contract_id: Canonical UTF-8 JSON with sorted keys, no whitespace and no NaN/Infinity, excluding top-level contract_id, golden_hash and expected_contract_id fields. The expected value lives in the adjacent golden sidecar.

stream_identity.server_stream_id: Random 64-bit lowercase hexadecimal value generated once per server process start.
stream_identity.scope: event_seq, subscriptions and reconnect high-water marks are scoped to server_stream_id.
stream_identity.restart_rule: A new server_stream_id invalidates resume positions; the client performs a new handshake and state snapshot.

counters.event_seq: Unsigned monotonic integer incremented exactly once for each committed broadcast event; it resets only with server_stream_id.
counters.stage_revision: Unsigned monotonic integer incremented once per committed logical state transaction.
counters.activation_generation: Unsigned monotonic integer incremented whenever a compiled active routing graph is atomically swapped.
counters.panic_generation: Unsigned monotonic integer incremented on the transition from non-panic to latched panic.

handshake.server_first: Immediately after accepting the socket, send server.hello with gate_state=awaiting_client.
handshake.client_rule: client.hello MUST echo this manifest's exact contract_id before subscriptions or commands are accepted.
handshake.match: An exact expected_contract_id match gates the connection ready. A mismatch leaves it incompatible and command-ineligible.
handshake.repeat: Repeat the full handshake whenever server_stream_id changes.

fields.request_id: Non-empty client-generated ID, unique for the lifetime of this socket and echoed by the response.
fields.payload: Object defined by the selected message type.

fields.event_seq: Current committed event high-water mark; only broadcast events increment it.
fields.sent_at_us: Unix time in integer microseconds for evidence correlation, never for ordering.
fields.request_id: Present on command.ack, command.error and the state.snapshot response; echoes the client request.
fields.payload: Object defined by the selected message type.

fields.channel: Exact source_id.channel registry address; the first dot is the separator.
fields.include_when: Optional object {channel, op, value}; channel is another exact registry address, op is eq|ne|lt|lte|gt|gte, and invalid predicate input excludes this value.

fields.bindings: Object binding every address-pattern placeholder to an in-bounds value.

variants.scale_range: Requires finite two-element in and out ranges plus boolean clamp.
variants.curve: Requires kind=linear|power|exponential|smoothstep|piecewise and the kind's finite parameters; piecewise points must be monotonic.
variants.smoothing: Requires kind=one_pole|ramp and finite non-negative time_ms.
variants.gate: Requires finite threshold and hysteresis, mode=level|rising_edge|falling_edge, and closed=suppress or {value: finite number}.
variants.combine: Must be first when route inputs has arity greater than one; operator=mean|sum|min|max|weighted_sum|difference and weights are required only for weighted_sum.

fields.held: accept|reject; accepted values remain held and retain confidence.
fields.hold_ms: Required bounded non-negative duration only for hold_then_reset.

fields.route_id: Stable lowercase identifier matching [a-z][a-z0-9_-]* within its scene.
fields.route_version: Positive integer incremented on every definition replacement.
fields.enabled: Boolean; disabled routes remain auditable and emit nothing.
fields.label: Optional operator-facing label.
fields.transforms: Ordered array of transform objects; arbitrary expressions or executable code are forbidden.
fields.notes: Optional design rationale; included in the scene hash.

fields.aggregator_id: Stable lowercase identifier within its scene.
fields.derived_source_id: Source registry identity produced by this declaration.
fields.output_channel: Single Source Frame-compatible channel name; address is derived_source_id.output_channel.
fields.inputs: Non-empty ordered array of exact channel_ref objects.
fields.cadence: Either {mode: fixed_hz, rate_hz: positive finite} or {mode: on_input, max_rate_hz: positive finite}.
fields.validity: Object requiring min_valid_count, min_observed_count, max_age_ms, include_held, held_max_ms and confidence=minimum|mean|product.
fields.contract_id_rule: The derived source's synthetic contract_id is the canonical hash of this complete declaration.

fields.duration_ms: Required non-negative duration for crossfade; scene default is 100.
fields.await_valid_ms: Bounded wait for crossfade; scene default is 250.
fields.hold_ms: Bounded wait for held; scene default is 250.
fields.destination_overrides: Optional array of exact destination plus transition; ambiguity is rejected.

fields.scene_version: Positive integer incremented on every scene transaction.
fields.name: Non-empty operator-facing name.
fields.created_at_us: Optional integer Unix microseconds; retained after creation.
fields.metadata: Optional JSON object containing declarative, non-executable metadata.

fields.instrument_contract_id: Exact Instrument Control contract ID to which this profile is bound.
fields.silence_actions: Ordered native actions/capability writes declared by the bound manifest; audio classes must reach silence within 20 ms.
fields.release_all_action: Required for polyphonic_instrument and must name a declared native action.
fields.force_all_off_action: Required for polyphonic_instrument and must name a declared native action.
fields.release_grace_ms: Required for polyphonic_instrument, finite and in [0,500].
fields.reset_defaults: Complete declared writable capability defaults needed by route reset and panic recovery.

payload.expected_contract_id: Exact 32-character contract ID from this manifest.

payload.after_event_seq: Optional reconnect hint valid only for the current server_stream_id; the server may still send a full snapshot.

payload.snapshot_event_seq: Atomic event high-water mark; subsequent events are strictly greater.
payload.stage: Object containing active_scene_id, activation_generation and panic state.
payload.routes: Selected persisted definitions and active runtime status when routes was requested.
payload.scenes: Complete scene definitions when scenes was requested.
payload.sources: Source registry snapshot when sources was requested.
payload.instruments: Instrument registry and safety status when instruments was requested.
payload.metrics: Current counters and latency summaries when metrics was requested.

payload.entity_id: Affected route or scene ID when applicable.
payload.entity: Committed entity or minimal runtime delta.
payload.activation_generation: Present when the active graph changed.

payload.route: Complete route entity whose route_version is 1.

route.create.atomicity: Validate and commit the scene plus active graph, if active, as one transaction.

payload.route_id: Existing route identity and must equal route.route_id.
payload.route: Complete replacement with route_version exactly expected_route_version + 1.

route.update.atomicity: Full replacement; JSON merge patch is not supported.

payload.expected_stage_revision: Compare-and-swap revision for the entire batch.
payload.operations: Non-empty ordered array of route.create|route.update|route.delete payloads without their expected_stage_revision.

route.batch.atomicity: Validate all operations against one candidate state, then commit all or none with one stage revision and at most one activation generation.

payload.expected_scene_version: Required for replacement and omitted for creation.
payload.scene: Complete scene entity; creation starts at version 1 and replacement increments exactly once.

payload.scene_id: Inactive target scene; deleting the active scene is rejected.

scene.switch.atomicity: Preflight and compile before a single tick-boundary activation; transition comes from the scene.

payload.idempotency_key: Optional stable retry key. Panic never requires expected_stage_revision.

panic.trigger.atomicity: Latch and close the route output gate before dispatching per-instrument actions.

payload.panic_generation: Must equal the active latched generation.
payload.scene_id: Explicit scene to rearm; there is no implicit resume.

panic.clear.atomicity: Revalidate contracts, state sync and safety profiles; reset route state and fade from safe values. Any failure leaves panic latched.

payload.reason: Failure or operator reason when applicable.

payload.source_id: Stable external or derived source identity.
payload.expected_contract_id: Installed or synthetic accepted contract ID.
payload.runtime_contract_id: Hello contract ID for external sources, when present.
payload.stream_id: Runtime source stream ID, when present.
payload.reason: Required for rejected, incompatible or absent transitions.

payload.expected_contract_id: Installed accepted Instrument Control contract ID.
payload.stream_id: Runtime instrument stream ID, when present.
payload.reason: Required for rejected, incompatible, absent or degraded transitions.

payload.activation_generation: Present when the active graph changed.

payload.command_type: Original command message type or unknown.
payload.details: Optional structured paths/reasons; must not contain secrets.

state_sync.authoritative_store: The server state store is authoritative; client-local state is only a projection.
state_sync.subscribe_transaction: Register the subscription, capture an immutable snapshot and its event high-water mark, send the snapshot, then send only later events.
state_sync.reconnect: A client normally requests a full snapshot. Resume is optional and valid only if server_stream_id matches and retained history is contiguous.
state_sync.commit_visibility: Broadcast definitions and runtime changes only after their state transaction commits.
state_sync.no_optimistic_client_truth: A client does not present a mutation as applied until command.ack or the matching committed event arrives.

registry_rules.source_install: Install an accepted Source Frame v1 manifest before connection; runtime hello cannot alter it.
registry_rules.source_gate: Accept frames only after source_id, stream_id and contract_id match; discard non-monotonic frames and invalidate all channels on lease expiry.
registry_rules.instrument_install: Install an accepted Instrument Control v1 manifest and a safety profile bound to its contract_id before performance arming.
registry_rules.instrument_gate: Send no writes until instrument_id and contract_id match and state synchronization completes for the current stream_id.
registry_rules.capability_gate: Only write=true declared capabilities, in-bounds bindings and declared arguments are patchable.
registry_rules.derived_gate: Compile an acyclic aggregator declaration and register its synthetic contract ID before routes can bind its output.

transaction_rules.serialization: The server serializes state mutations and compare-and-swap checks.
transaction_rules.preflight: Validate identifiers, finite values, manifests, capabilities, graph cycles, transform arity, destination uniqueness, defaults and safety before commit.
transaction_rules.activation: Active graph changes swap once at an engine tick; no client observes a partially compiled generation.
transaction_rules.failure: A rejected mutation leaves persisted state and the active graph unchanged.
transaction_rules.one_writer: At most one enabled route may target one fully bound destination argument in a scene.
transaction_rules.audit: Persist the complete declarative transaction and resulting revisions; executable route content is forbidden.

panic_semantics.priority: panic.trigger bypasses normal mutation order, latches state and closes output before instrument I/O.
panic_semantics.latched_state: Keep sources and telemetry live, retain scene edits, suppress all writes and discard triggers without queueing.
panic_semantics.sustained_processor: Native panic if declared, then silence gains/enables within 20 ms; reset other controls while silence stays pinned.
panic_semantics.polyphonic_instrument: Block note-ons, release all, silence master within 20 ms, force all off within at most 500 ms, then reset while master stays pinned.
panic_semantics.control_only: Suppress events and reset declared controls.
panic_semantics.partial_failure: Continue other instruments, report per-instrument outcome, remain latched and retry safety on reconnect.
panic_semantics.recovery: Only panic.clear with the current generation and explicit valid scene can recover; restore defaults and fade from safe values for at least 250 ms.
panic_semantics.no_replay: Never replay events received or queued before recovery.

weaver_overhead_target_ms.scope: Local source receipt to instrument send under the full MVP route set; provisional T4.2 acceptance target.

latency_and_evidence.intentional_time_exclusions: Report fixed cadence wait, scene crossfade and instrument-declared lag separately; do not hide them inside Weaver overhead.
required_evidence: run configuration and all accepted contract IDs
latency_summary.json with sample count and p50/p95/p99
behavior events for gating, validity, atomic scene switch, panic and recovery

mvp_scope.required_performance_case: Focused HarMoCAP subject controls one harmonic per limb plus head; non-focus slots aggregate into the harmonic bed; scenes switch live and global panic is recoverable.

receiver_rules.unknown_type: Return command.error code=bad_message; do not mutate state.
receiver_rules.contract_mismatch: Allow only handshake traffic; do not subscribe or mutate.
receiver_rules.revision_conflict: Return the current stage revision and require a fresh snapshot or explicit retry.
receiver_rules.panic_priority: Process panic.trigger even when its client view is stale and even while another mutation awaits commit.
receiver_rules.panic_latch: Reject route activation and scene.switch while panic is active; route/scene definition edits may commit but emit nothing.
receiver_rules.finite_numbers: Reject NaN, Infinity, out-of-range values and numeric booleans everywhere.
receiver_rules.event_order: Use server_stream_id plus event_seq, never timestamps, for event ordering.
receiver_rules.thin_client: A UI must reconstruct its projection from state.snapshot plus later events and must not bypass this protocol to control instruments.
